steps.title: Identify the tense anchor
steps.content: The dependent clause “When we arrived in Venice, Utah” uses simple past tense. The main clause verb must match: both arriving and noticing the geoglyph happened at the same completed moment in the past. The rule of verb tense consistency requires that all verbs in the same narrative sequence share the same tense unless a shift is specifically justified.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — notice: Simple present tense — violates tense consistency with the past-tense dependent clause “when we arrived.” ✗
  • Choice B — are noticing: Present progressive tense — also present tense; conflicts with the past setting established by “arrived.” ✗
  • Choice C — will notice: Simple future tense — cannot describe an action that occurred simultaneously with a past event. ✗
  • Choice D — noticed: Simple past tense — matches “arrived,” correctly showing both actions occurred together in the past. ✓
That is the correct answer.
